author: y4rr
content:
The base sharded loader was already implemented in \`ferrotorch-serialize/src/safetensors_io.rs\` — \`load_safetensors_sharded\`, \`load_safetensors_auto\`, full \`SafeTensorsIndex\` parsing of \`model.safetensors.index.json\`, deterministic shard order, and a Llama-3-8B integration test. This commit adds the two missing UX pieces.

## What landed
### \`load_safetensors_sharded_with_progress\`
Per-shard progress callback. Useful for HF-style progress bars on huge models (Llama 3 70B is ~140 GB across many shards). Each invocation receives:
- shard_index / shard_count
- shard_file (no directory prefix)
- tensors_in_shard
- tensors_loaded_so_far (cumulative across previous shards)
- total_tensors (from \`weight_map.len()\`)

The callback is purely advisory — return value ignored. Callers can drive progress bars, structured logging, or HUB telemetry from it.

### \`load_safetensors_sharded_filtered\`
Predicate-driven selective load. Pass a \`Fn(&str) -> bool\` to choose which tensor names to deserialize. Shards with no matches are skipped entirely (no I/O, no decode), so loading "only layers 12..16" or "only the LM head" is fast on large checkpoints.

## Tests (3 new in \`safetensors_io::tests\`)
- \`sharded_loader_progress_callback_fires_per_shard\` — verifies per-shard ordering and that the cumulative count is correct
- \`sharded_loader_filter_keeps_only_matching_keys\` — predicate selects across two shards
- \`sharded_loader_filter_skips_shard_with_no_matches\` — empty-match shard is silently skipped

19 safetensors tests pass total. Workspace tests: zero regressions.

## Re-exports
\`ferrotorch-serialize/src/lib.rs\` now re-exports \`ShardProgress\`, \`load_safetensors_auto\`, \`load_safetensors_sharded\`, \`load_safetensors_sharded_with_progress\`, \`load_safetensors_sharded_filtered\` (previously only \`load_safetensors\` and \`save_safetensors\` were public).

The Llama-3 weights ingestion path through \`ferrotorch-llama\` already calls \`load_safetensors_sharded\`; consumers wanting progress can now upgrade by switching the call site.
